summary:
Great! The tests have passed. Let's summarize what we did to fix the issue:

1. We identified that the problem was that DataLoader instances were being created inside each resolver call instead of at the request level, which prevented batching from working.

2. We moved the DataLoader creation to the context factory (createContext) in context.ts, so that all resolvers within a single request share the same loader instances.

3. We updated the userResolver.ts and postResolver.ts to use the shared DataLoader instances from the context instead of creating new ones in each resolver call.

4. We ran the test script, which verified that our fix worked by showing that the query count is now within acceptable bounds (2 queries instead of 101).

The key insight was that DataLoader batching relies on all keys being enqueued on the same loader instance within the same tick. By creating a new loader for each resolver call, we were preventing batching from happening.

This fix ensures that DataLoader batching works correctly, which will significantly improve the performance of GraphQL queries that fetch related data.
